TextENGNew: ``Okinomiya PS to all units.@k@vS09/00/VTP_OkeijiA_1002. Okinomiya PS to all units.@k@vS09/00/VTP_OkeijiA_1003. A barricade incident has occurred at the Hinamizawa Forestry Service.@k@vS09/00/VTP_OkeijiA_1004. Multiple hostages reported.@k@vS09/00/VTP_OkeijiA_1005. All units proceed to the Hinamizawa Forestry Service immediately.``
changeReason: Old says 'hostage situation' but 篭城事件 means 'barricade incident/siege.'

TextENGNew: ``Kuma-chan!!@k@vS09/11/170700309. Sorry I'm late!@k@vS09/11/170700340. What's the situation?!``
``Welcome, sir!@k@vS09/19/173100072. Today at approximately 1:00 PM, a classroom occupation occurred at the Hinamizawa branch school section of the Hinamizawa Forestry Service building.``
changeReason: New translation adds location detail not in original: 'forestry service'.

TextENGNew: ``The suspect's initial demands are to prohibit all entry to the forestry service grounds and to establish a hotline to us.``
changeReason: Different locations referenced: old mentions 'branch school', new mentions 'forestry service'.

TextENGNew: ``...So they intend to negotiate with us from the outset?@k@vS09/11/170700312. ...What's their game...?``
changeReason: Different implication: 'What's going on?' is neutral inquiry while 'What's their game?' implies suspicion about deliberate scheming.

TextENGNew: ``...Also, the suspect has requested you specifically as negotiator, Ooishi-san.@k@vS09/19/173100076. The suspect is Reina Ryuugu.``
``Ryuugu-san...?!``
``...Very well.@k@vS09/11/170700315. It seems we're bound by fate at this point.@k@vS09/11/170700316. I'll see this through to the end.``
Ooishi gave a bitter smile.
changeReason: Different characterization: 'crooked smile' vs 'bitter smile' (JPN 苦々しく = bitter).

TextENGNew: ``It's unknown whether there are any accomplices besides Reina Ryuugu.@k@vS09/19/173100078. The curtains have been drawn, so we've been unable to confirm the situation inside.``
``Have you spoken to the person who reported the incident about the conditions during the takeover?``
``No, it was a direct statement from Reina Ryuugu herself.@k@vS09/19/173100080. Therefore, we have been unable to confirm the internal situation or the condition of the hostages.``
``...She planned this carefully, didn't she.@k@vS09/11/170700319. Any forestry service personnel among the hostages?``

``The forestry service staff were absent today.@k@vS09/19/173100083. So it's just the twenty-five students.@k@vS09/19/173100084. No teachers, either -- the principal was away, and the homeroom teacher was apparently lured away as well.``
changeReason: Numerical quantities differ: old has {24}, new has {25}.

TextENGNew: ``So it's just the suspect and the students.@k@vS09/11/170700321. Any other suspicious figures spotted besides Reina Ryuugu?``

``No, sir. There have been occasional glimpses of a figure peeking through the curtains, but each time it was confirmed as Ryuugu Reina herself.@k@vS09/19/173100086. ...However, considering the scale and other factors, we estimate there are at least one or two more people inside.``
"......Rena-chan is such a serious, good girl...!@k@vS09/22/171400073. For her to do something like this... there must be some mistake...!@k@vS09/22/171400074. Ooohhh!"
"A-Are you all right...?"
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content with different character references, indicating a misaligned passage.

TextENGNew: Ooishi hurried over to Chie-sensei, who had broken down crying with a handkerchief pressed to her eyes...
Chie-sensei had sunk down completely, and it seemed impossible to get anything more out of her.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content with different character references, indicating a misaligned passage.

TextENGNew: ``Ooishi-san...!@k@vS09/19/173100091. It's the principal and the homeroom teacher!``
"Principal, let me do that!!@k@vS09/22/171400076. It's all my fault, ahhhhhhhhh...!!"
"Teachers, please don't blame yourselves.@k@vS09/11/170700329. ...I'll need to ask you about the situation inside the school, so please cooperate with us."
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content with different character references, indicating a misaligned passage.

TextENGNew: "Bonchi-kun!@k@vS09/11/170700331. Please take them to the floor plans and hear what they have to say."
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned passage.

TextENGNew: "Excuse me!@k@vS09/19/173100089. Section Chief Takasugi is on Car 1's radio!"
"Yes, yes, hello... this is Ooishi."

"Ooishi-san, good work out there!@k@vS09/00/taka17004. Kumagai-kun filled me in on the general situation.@k@vS09/00/taka17005. The suspect is a minor, I hear?@k@vS09/00/taka17006. Make sure you keep strict control over the press!"
"Understood.@k@vS09/11/170700334. ......Also, it seems the suspect has designated me as negotiator. Is that all right?"
``Ooishi-san...!@k@vS09/19/173100093. On the car phone... it's the suspect, Reina Ryuugu...!``
"Ooishi-san......!@k@vS09/19/173100091. The suspect—Ryuugu Rena—is on the car phone......!"

TextENGNew: "...I've already gotten a call from the requested party.@k@vS09/11/170700337. I'll take it now."

TextENGNew: ``...........Thank you.@k@vS09/11/170700339. ......Hello, this is Ooishi.``

TextENGNew: He clenched his teeth in anger and frustration, balling his fists tightly.

TextENGNew: An overwhelming wall of police officers blocked my way, and I could feel pathetic for only being able to watch the teachers and the detectives from afar.
The reason behind why Chie-sensei had been phoned and summoned out of the school.
...For Rena who wanted to hijack to classroom, the biggest obstacle was Chie-sensei.
She was someone who had a strong sense of justice.
Even if Rena had held a pistol to her, Chie-sensei probably wouldn't have hesitated.
For the welfare of her students, she would put herself in danger... getting rid of someone like her was the primary step in order to hijack the classroom.
Attacking her from behind would have been her very first line of thought but,@k I'm sure she decided taking on an adult directly was a risky affair.
...In that case, it was better to lure her away to someplace..!
Rena had then phoned Chie-sensei, and then told her she wished to seek her advice in someplace private... and asked to meet her near that suspension bridge.
Including Chie-sensei, a good majority of the people thought that Rena had merely run away from home.
...So Chie-sensei probably thought Rena merely had some troubles she wished to discuss.@k ......Fooling her was that easy.
So... where did Rena make the call from...?
Yes, it was from within the school.@k Well, not exactly.
It was most likely from the second floor of the school building, .......from the office room of the Forestry Service.
The Forestry Service at our school was more like a branch office, so it wasn't like the staff were always present.@k Depending on the day, they might not even be there.
Rena had known this, and had been hiding on the second floor all this time.
And then, she had made a call to the ground floor from up there, ......and sent Chie-sensei to a faraway place.
It probably wasn't a coincidence that the principal had also been away on a business trip today.
......Rena had most likely snuck into the school yesterday itself.
And then, she had discerned the principal's itinerary and the workings of the internal phone line before she put her plan into action.
......How cunning it all was......
......No, I understood.
...Right now, even as she was possessed by a murderous rage, ...Rena was keeping her composure.
...She was unbelievably calm despite being plagued by her delusion that controlled her.
...And so, just thinking about it made my head spin.
...Because I had even personally experienced what she was going through before.
Also, what was even more unlucky was the absence of the Sonozaki Family bodyguard who had been standing by the gate.
......It was because Mion had sent him to follow Chie-sensei who had set out to meet Rena.
.........Fuck, everything was so screwed up... on so many levels.........

``We're trying to come up with a plan for the simultaneous raids as we speak. We're talking to the prefectural police as well as the Public Safety Division.@k@vS09/11/170700375. We're talking about the entire city, so we need a little more time to prepare for it...``
``You're taking too long!!!@k@vS09/02/170201483. Ooishi-san, don't you realize what kind of emergency situation Shishibone City is in right now?

What are you doing?@k@vS09/02/170201485. Ooishi-san...... you do believe me, don't you?@k@vS09/02/170201486. You're my ally, right?@k@vS09/02/170201487. Right?@k@vS09/02/170201488. Please answer me!!!``

``O-Of course, I am!@k@vS09/11/170700377. But the police department is a little more complicated than you think...@k@vS09/11/170700378. I'm doing my best, I really am!@k@vS09/11/170700379. The thing is this is on a really big scale...``
...The negotiations weren't that satisfactory, to be honest.
Even as they spoke, it was clear from the overwhelming tension that Rena's patience was wearing thinner and thinner.
``...Okay, call Keiichi-kun over here.``
``What?@k@vS09/11/430500002. Could you say that again?``
``Isn't Keiichi-kun there right now?@k@vS09/02/430400003. I'd like to talk to him now so please call him over immediately!``
``...I-I understand.``
Ooishi asked one of his subordinates to summon the boy who stood in the front line of the crowd gathered at the barricade.
He immediately jogged over to the car as soon as he was called.
``You must be Keiichi Maebara, I assume?``
``Miss Rena Ryuugu would like to have a word with you.@k@vS09/11/430500006. Could you please take the call?``
``...I understand.``
Ooishi then handed over the receiver to Keiichi and he noticed something happening outside and left the car.
``Ah, Keiichi-kun...?@k@vS09/02/430400005. It's good that you came back here.``

``The police isn't believing what I'm telling them.@k@vS09/02/430400007. Could you please tell them that the things I'm saying are true, and that I'm very serious about what I'm about to do... could you?``
``.........@k@vS09/01/430200039. What do you mean by... 'what I'm about to do'...?``

``I've just finished pouring gasoline all over this classroom.@k@vS09/02/430400009. So, if I just ignite the lighter I have on hand, all the hostages here will die.@k@vS09/02/430400010. ...Could you please let Ooishi-san know about that?``
``Wh-Who are you people?!``
``Move over.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1002. Who's in charge here?``
Several cars had arrived, producing several men. They weren't from the Okinomiya station.
One of the men was wearing a suit, despite the hot weather. He approached me, pushing Kumagai aside.
``...Hey, look who's here.@k@vS09/11/170700523. Good to see you again, Ootaka-kun from the prefectural headquarters.``
``Watch your mouth.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1004. I'm ranked much higher than you now, so don't address me so casually.``

``Can I help you with something, Ootaka-kun?@k@|@y@vS09/11/170700525. I'm rather busy at the moment.@k@vS09/11/170700526. You can't possibly be thinking about taking over this case?@k@vS09/11/170700527. Who approved that, exactly?``
``I did, Ooishi-kun!``
``Ch-Chief...?``
``Takasugi-kun has informed me of the situation.@k@vS09/00/syocho17003. He also told me that you started all this.@k@vS09/00/syocho17004. You can go now. The troops will take over!``
``The troops? ...Ootaka-kun, you aren't going to storm the building, are you?``

``I understand the risk.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1006. But we don't have any time left before the bomb goes off.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1007. You've taken too long.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1008. We're here to take care of the mess you left behind.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1009. Just step back for a minute.``
I couldn't talk back if both the chief and a guy from the prefectural headquarters gave me an order.
``O...Ooishi-san, who the heck is he?``
``That's Ootaka-kun.@k@vS09/11/170700531. Nfu fu, a long time ago, he was showing off even though he was bad at mahjong, so I taught him a lesson.
Or maybe he's still holding a grudge from when I knocked him unconscious in that kendo tournament.``
Obviously, we didn't get along.
``Give me the latest update, Ooishi-kun.``
``Are you serious about storming the school?``
``A special unit from the prefectural police department will handle the infiltration.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1012. Your unit will be their backup.``
``Ooishi-kun,@k@vS09/00/syocho17006. the special unit from the prefectural police department isn't just any old unit.@k@vS09/00/syocho17007. You've heard about the Second Company, haven't you?``
``What's that, Ooishi-san?@k@vS09/19/173100174. Is this ``Second Company`` that well known?``

``Do you remember, about five years ago, when the JRA hijacked that airplane? Flight 472?@k@vS09/11/170700548. I've heard that special counter-terrorist units were put together in Tokyo and Osaka after that incident.@k@vS09/11/170700549. So they really do exist...``
``B-But why would a special unit from Osaka be here?``
Ootaka smiled proudly.
``We just happened to be holding a training session with your prefectural police unit.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1014. We requested their help.``
``Na ha ha... you haven't changed, have you?@k@|@y@vS09/11/170700551. ...You have a bad tendency to escalate matters quickly.``
The Company members exited the vehicles one after another and took position.
They didn't move like policemen...
``Ootaka-kun!@k@vS09/11/170700553. Are you really going to have a gunfight here?``

``Don't worry.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1016. They won't use firearms.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1017. They're using riot shields and riot guns.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1018. These guns send out tear gas at high pressures.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1019. There will be no issue with explosions.``
``......Wow, I've never heard of such convenient water guns.@k@vS09/11/170700556. By the way, can that toy reach the classroom from across the schoolyard?``
``You don't need to worry about that.@k@vS09/00/VT3_ootaka1021. You just move over and let us do our job.``
And then, following Ootaka's orders, the special forces took positions at a blind spot visible from the school window and prepared to infiltrate.

Ooishi wrinkled his nose at Ootaka's arrogance and pretentious attitude that didn't give way for counterarguments. Remembering that Rena and Keiichi were having a conversation, he turned on his heel heading over to the patrol car.
And there...
``Detective, we have an emergency!!``
At once, the door to the car swung open and Keiichi rushed over to him screaming.
``What's the matter, Maebara-san?``
``Re-Rena just told me she's poured gasoline all over the classroom...!!``
``Whaaa?!``
Gasoline...?! I didn't hear that there was something so inflammable in the vicinity!! No, it's not about that, right now—!!
``Preparing to shoot, steady—``
``P-Please wait!@k@vS09/11/430500010. Stop, don't shoot!!``
``Uwaaaahhhh!!``
Our bodies were promptly blown away and hit the ground, we didn't understand what had happened for a moment.
A high pitched tinnitus violently reverberated within my skull, and an overwhelming dizziness distanced me from my consciousness.
My throat and nasal passage felt irritated from the suspended dust particles and smoldered air.
Spitting out the crunchy debris from my mouth, I dragged my beaten body up into a stance, and faced forward, looking ahead of me.
There were many police officers scattered about on the ground, groaning from the impact.
...A voice of agony filled the air next to me. ...As I faced it, it turned out to be the detective I'd been talking to just now.
His entire body was covered in dirt and debris, and he was bleeding from his head.
As I began to sit down in concern for him, I suddenly remembered Mion and Rena's safety.
And then, I turned towards the direction of the school.
...I@k ...couldn't believe@k ...what I was seeing.
What stood before me was a disordered conglomerate of wood, bricks and other distorted articles mixed in... crudely resembling an enormous camping bonfire.
The flames that had risen up in between that mass burned so high they seemed to reach up into the evening twilight.
Even from this faraway distance, I could perceive the heat and the scorching smell.
No, this had to be a dream.
This couldn't be our school.
No, this wasn't even a school in the first place...
I staggered over.
Fumbling forward with my useless legs, I walked over to that monstrosity.
And then before me, amidst the crumbled remains of timber, I discovered a face I recognized and drew a breath before I rushed over.
Mion's eyes were wide open, facing my way, with her right arm raised.
Her mouth was slightly open... she probably couldn't speak.
``Thank god... Mion...!``
I had to get her out here first!
Thinking that, I tightly grasped that right arm—.
It felt light, there was no resistance.@k And then, from within the rubble,@k@|@y 'it' came out.
And then, a small mass rolled over,@k and touched my feet.
Mi@w600.on's@w600. head@w600. had@w600. co@w600.me@w600. a@w600.part@w600. from@w600. her@w600. body@w600....
``U-Uwaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhhhh!!@k@vS09/01/430200059. Aaaaahhhhhhhhh!!!!``
